mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates directly into higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or the interaction term predictive IC drops below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening economic momentum and capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate profit expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of recovery phase observations, or the 5-day return correlation with INDPRO growth falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate signal an active monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns exceed 55% of triggered observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day horizons or fails to outperform a buy-and-hold baseline in walk-forward backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real gross domestic product confirms an economic downturn, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: The market fails to price in the contraction, with positive 5-day returns occurring in more than 70% of negative GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in industrial output and employment indicates a widening positive output gap, which drives higher corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when either series decouples, or the combined IC falls below 0.02 across rolling 3-year evaluation windows.
